rulings.Case Title: MERCEDES ZORAIDA HEGLAND, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Nonparty Honeywell, Inc'S Production Of Documents Or Tangible Things
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel nonparty Honeywell, Inc.¿s production of documents or tangible items is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GERALDINE HILT et al VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Objections, Compel Substantive Verified Responses To Requests For Admission, Form Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions From Deft Sheedy Drayage Co.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to strike objections and to compel further responses to requests for admission and form interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. Within 20 days defendant must provide further responses to requests for admissions 2-8, 12-22 and further responses to interrogatory 17.1 as to requests for admissions 1, 9-11 and 23-24. Defendant¿s responses to requests for admission 1, 9-11 and 23-24 are sufficient, yet defendant¿s responses to form interrogatory 17.1 as to those responses are insufficient. The responses to the requests for admissions are sufficient because they contain denials with satisfactory non-evasive explanations why defendant made those denials and/or contain adequate statements of insufficient knowledge to admit or deny the requests. The responses to 17.1 are insufficient because defendant has not answered any of the subparts of that interrogatory as to those responses and its responses to the requests for admissions are not an adequate substitute for answering the subparts of 17.1. Defendant¿s responses to requests for admission 2-8, 12-22 are insufficient because those responses either include evasive or non-responsive information and/or were responded to under the mistaken assumption that an inability to admit or deny after reasonable inquiry is a permissible basis for a denial. Plaintiffs¿ motion to strike defendant¿s general objections is granted, but plaintiffs¿ motion to strike specific objections is denied. The general objections lack merit and are not in proper form. The specific objections were timely made and are preserved for trial. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. Defendant¿s position did not lack substantial justification. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Coleman Company, Inc' Responses To General Order 129 Standard Interrogatories To Defts
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff¿s interrogatories from defendant Coleman Company was continued per agreement of the parties from September 6 to September 20. If all issues raised by the motion are not resolved before then, by 4:30pm on September 19, 2011 the parties are required to send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain unresolved and then appear at the hearing at 9am on September 20. = (220/HEK)
